presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
The Access Review Evidence Log and the Privileged Account Approval Email address distinct control objectives: the former documents evidence collection for a vendor control review with a focus on provenance verification to close the control, while the latter establishes conditional, scoped privileged access for specific administrators subject to strict logging, quarterly recertification, and revalidation upon personnel changes. The evidence log emphasizes completion of evidence items to achieve control closure, whereas the email mandates ongoing compliance through periodic review and vendor confirmation to sustain access.

- Evidence log requires verification of all evidence items for control closure; email mandates quarterly recertification and revalidation on personnel changes.  
- Evidence log notes missing/incomplete items blocking closure; email requires strict task scoping and logging compliance for access.  
- Evidence log is synthetic/test-only; email is an operational approval with vendor confirmation requirements.  
- Evidence log tracks statuses (received, missing, in progress); email specifies standing access for named administrators.  
- Evidence log references repository locations; email mandates logging and vendor confirmation for access maintenance.  
- Evidence log closure depends on provenance; email requires quarterly review to maintain access.
key_highlights: The Access Review Evidence Log and the Privileged Account Approval Email address distinct control objectives: the former documents evidence collection for a vendor control review with a focus on provenance verification to close the control, while the latter establishes conditional, scoped privileged access for specific administrators subject to strict logging, quarterly recertification, and revalidation upon personnel changes. The evidence log emphasizes completion of evidence items to achieve control closure, whereas the email mandates ongoing compliance through periodic review and vendor confirmation to sustain access.
Evidence log requires verification of all evidence items for control closure; email mandates quarterly recertification and revalidation on personnel changes.
Evidence log notes missing/incomplete items blocking closure; email requires strict task scoping and logging compliance for access.
Evidence log is synthetic/test-only; email is an operational approval with vendor confirmation requirements.
Evidence log tracks statuses (received, missing, in progress); email specifies standing access for named administrators.
Evidence log references repository locations; email mandates logging and vendor confirmation for access maintenance.
